STRENGTH.notes: Heavy compound lifts prioritize low repetitions for maximal force production.

HYPERTROPHY.notes: Mechanical tension and progressive overload are the primary drivers.

WEIGHT_LOSS.notes: Moderate repetitions maximize calorie expenditure while preserving strength.

GENERAL_FITNESS.notes: Balanced loading for long-term health and movement quality.

STRENGTH.notes: Isolation exercises support compound strength but are rarely trained maximally.

HYPERTROPHY.notes: Higher repetitions reduce joint stress while maximizing muscle stimulus.

ENDURANCE.notes: Isolation movements tolerate higher repetitions well.

WEIGHT_LOSS.notes: Higher density training improves caloric expenditure.

GENERAL_FITNESS.notes: Comfortable repetition ranges for most populations.

STRENGTH.notes: Core strength emphasizes controlled bracing over excessive repetitions.

HYPERTROPHY.notes: Higher repetitions are appropriate for abdominal hypertrophy.

ENDURANCE.notes: Core endurance benefits from prolonged time under tension.

STRENGTH.notes: Heavy loading is effective due to the calf's strength capacity.

HYPERTROPHY.notes: Calves generally respond well to higher repetitions.

WEIGHT_LOSS.notes: Useful as accessory work in higher-density sessions.

STRENGTH.notes: Power movements should prioritize speed and quality over fatigue.

HYPERTROPHY.notes: Explosive exercises are generally not primary hypertrophy movements.

ENDURANCE.notes: Explosive lifts lose effectiveness with excessive fatigue.

GENERAL_FITNESS.notes: Focus on movement quality and power development.

STRENGTH.notes: Heavy carries improve grip, trunk stiffness and work capacity.
